title: PHP Strings
content:
### PHP Strings

A string is a sequence of characters enclosed in quotes.

### String Quotes

| Type | Syntax | Variable Parsing |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| Single quotes | `'Hello'` | No |
| Double quotes | `"Hello"` | Yes |
| Heredoc | `<<<EOT ... EOT;` | Yes |
| Nowdoc | `<<<'EOT' ... EOT;` | No |

```php
<?php
$name = "John";

// Single quotes - no variable parsing
echo 'Hello $name'; // Hello $name

// Double quotes - parses variables
echo "Hello $name"; // Hello John

// Heredoc
echo <<<EOT
Multiline string
with $name
EOT;
?>
```

### String Concatenation

Use the `.` operator to join strings:

```php
<?php
$greeting = "Hello" . " " . "World";
$greeting .= "!";
echo $greeting; // Hello World!
?>
```

> **Key Point:** Double-quoted strings parse variables and escape sequences like `\n` (newline) and `\t` (tab).
codeExample:
<?php
$firstName = "Jane";
$lastName = "Doe";
$full = "$firstName $lastName";
echo $full; // Jane Doe
echo strlen($full); // 8
echo strtoupper($full); // JANE DOE
?>

quiz.question: Which quote type allows variable parsing?
What operator is used for string concatenation?
